theme: Every pixel has a job — audit the quiet corners and make them earn their rent
visual_observations: Desktop: office floor glows purple (crushing vibe), Bloberto crowned center-top as CVO, all five workers visible at desks, coffee corner reads 'COFFEE TRIPLE SHOT' (static text), orange gradient sky window top-right labeled tiny 'dusk', conference table ring sits empty in bottom-left, fern + cactus bottom-right corner, 'everyone is at their desk' footer text barely visible. Dashboard: 5 stat cards — ON THE CLOCK (1), ACTIVE (1 'lone wolf'), IDLE (0 'all hands on deck'), LIVE timestamp ticking, TEAM VIBE sparkline; Sofia's card shows 10% progress, '<1m' working time, QA task tag, but no ETA. Mobile (375px): desks shrink to ~40px wide, nameplates completely illegible, only 'SOFIA' label is barely readable, 'DUSK' label on window more prominent than on desktop, conference ring floats as an unlabeled ghost, vibe streak badge absent at this viewport. Across all views: the footer quote counter '1/8' is nearly invisible, Bloberto's crown is static gold regardless of vibe intensity.

ideas.title: Office Window Is Color Blind to the Vibe
ideas.description: The OfficeWindow component in Office.jsx uses getGradient(hour) for a purely time-driven sky — the VIBE_WEATHER_LABELS object already maps vibes to weather descriptions but nothing flows into the window. Pass vibeKey as a prop to OfficeWindow and blend a vibe-specific CSS variable into the sky: on-fire tints the gradient red-orange (overlay rgba(239,68,68,0.15)), crushing adds violet shimmer (rgba(139,92,246,0.2)), slow-day desaturates with grey wash (filter: saturate(0.4)), after-hours deepens indigo. This matters because the window is the first decorative element users see upper-right — right now it tells you it's dusk, not that the team is on fire. Two props, three CSS variables, zero new components.

ideas.title: Progress Bar Tells You Where, Not When — Add the ETA
ideas.description: In App.jsx, ProgressBar receives startedAt and progress. When progress > 5% and startedAt is valid, the remaining time is trivial math: const pct = progress / 100; const elapsed = Date.now() - new Date(startedAt); const etaMins = Math.round((elapsed / pct - elapsed) / 60000). Render a .progress-eta span beside the percentage (e.g. '~8m left') in muted text. When progress hits 100, show '✓ done'. Why it matters: Sofia sitting at 10% with '<1m elapsed' tells users nothing about whether to panic or relax. An ETA converts a progress bar from a 'where' indicator into a 'when' forecast — the single most anxious question people have watching live work.

ideas.title: Mobile Desks Are Braille — Give Fingers a Voice
ideas.description: At 375px viewport, desk nameplates are 0.6rem and desk containers shrink to roughly 40px wide — completely illegible. Office.jsx's Character already detects touch via window.matchMedia('(hover: none)'). Add a two-tap behavior for mobile: first tap renders a .char-mobile-popup absolutely-positioned callout (above the character, z-index above office floor) showing worker emoji, name, role, and StatusBadge. A second tap (or tap elsewhere) navigates to the dashboard worker card via onClick. Currently, one tap skips straight to the dashboard — mobile users lose all floor context and can't identify who they're even tapping. This is a discovery and comprehension failure, not just a cosmetic issue.

ideas.title: The Lone Ranger Has No Spotlight on the Floor
ideas.description: When activeWorkers.length === 1, App.jsx renders the .workers-grid--solo layout and the solo-spotlight-msg ('🌟 Running solo today. Carrying the whole team on one pair of hands.'). But the Office floor doesn't know. The lone active worker just sits at their desk identically to any other day. Pass an isSoloChampion boolean from App into Office, then into the lone worker's Character component where it adds a .char--solo-champion class: a gold pulsing halo shadow (box-shadow: 0 0 18px rgba(251,191,36,0.5), animation: solo-glow 2s ease-in-out infinite). The floor should echo the dashboard's hero narrative — right now the two views tell completely different emotional stories about the same moment.

ideas.title: Task Tags Live in the Dashboard Basement — Surface Them on the Floor
ideas.description: getTaskTags() in App.jsx parses task strings and returns typed tag objects (Bug 🔴, Ship 🟠, Design 🟣, QA 🔵, Data 🟤, Docs 🟢). These render as colorful badges in WorkerCard. On the Office floor, Character receives a tooltip prop that currently shows 'Name · Role'. Since activityEntries is already passed to Character, and the worker object has a task field, add the first matching tag's emoji+label to the tooltip: 'Sofia · QA · 🔵 QA' instead of 'Sofia · QA'. One-line change to the tooltip construction in Office.jsx. Why it matters: the floor view is the first thing visitors see — task context without having to navigate to the dashboard makes the office feel like a real working space, not just a roster.

ideas.title: Coffee Corner Shot Strength Should Match the Crowd
ideas.description: The .coffee-corner label currently shows 'COFFEE TRIPLE SHOT' as a static string in the rendered JSX (Office.jsx). The coffee-corner--chatting and coffee-corner--solo states exist — but the shot strength never changes. Map crowd size to espresso intensity: 0 workers = 'DECAF' (grey), 1 worker = 'SINGLE SHOT' (warm), 2 workers = 'DOUBLE SHOT' (amber), 3+ workers = 'TRIPLE SHOT' (gold glow). Store the label computation next to the existing chatBadge logic. This turns a charming but inert decoration into a subtle real-time social metric. Users will start checking the corner to gauge team gathering momentum without reading a single stat.

ideas.title: The Footer Quote Counter Is a Secret Menu — Give It One Wink
ideas.description: The footer renders a quote counter like '2/8' at opacity 0.55 in tiny tabular-nums — invisible unless you're hunting for it. The footer-sparkle--twinkle animation already fires for first-time visitors via localStorage flag 'footer-sparkle-seen'. During that same first-visit window, add a .footer-hint span that appears for 4s then fades out: 'click ✦ for more'. After that, let it be secret again. Add title='Click for next quote' to the .footer-tagline wrapper. This preserves the easter-egg magic for returning users while giving new visitors one gentle nudge. The quote cycling is currently the most delightful invisible feature — it deserves a single moment of discovery.

ideas.title: Bloberto's Crown Should Chronicle Chaos — Make It Earn Its Shine
ideas.description: In CharacterAvatar (Office.jsx), Bloberto's crown is a hardcoded yellow polygon fill='#fbbf24' regardless of actual team state. The crown sits at the highest visual point of the entire floor — it should be the first signal. Implement crown state as a computed fill and optional SVG animation: on-fire → fill='#ef4444' with <animateTransform type='rotate'> slight quiver (±2deg, 0.3s); crushing → fill='#fbbf24' with a <animate> on strokeWidth pulsing from 0 to 1 on a white stroke (sparkle effect); slow-day → fill='#78716c' (pewter, dim and defeated); after-hours → fill='#6366f1' (indigo glow matching floor border); in-flow → current gold. vibeKey is already passed to CharacterAvatar as a prop — this is pure SVG animation, no new state, no props changes. The crown is Bloberto's emotional barometer. Right now it lies.
